verbGlossary.CONTAINS: Parent {target} includes the target as a component part — the target exists within the source's structure (e.g., a drawing set contains individual sheets, a daily report contains log notes)
verbGlossary.REVISED BY: {source} document has a newer version issued as the target — {target}
verbGlossary.SUPERSEDES: {source} revision replaces the target as the current version — all parties must work from the source after issuance, the target is no longer {target} of record
verbGlossary.ACCOMPANIED BY: {source} document is issued together with {target}ual package (e.g., drawings accompanied by specifications)
verbGlossary.REQUIRES: {source} document mandates that {target} be produced — a contractual or specification-driven requirement for submittal or deliverable (e.g., specification section requires a submittal)
verbGlossary.MODIFIES: {source} {target} formally changes the content of the target — the target's requirements, scope, or details are altered by the source (e.g., addendum modifies drawings or specs before contract execution)
verbGlossary.CLARIFIES: {source} {target} provides additional detail or interpretation of the target without formally changing it — resolves ambiguity in the target's content
verbGlossary.SUPPLEMENTS: {source} {target} adds information to the target without replacing it — extends or elaborates on the target's content as a companion document
verbGlossary.ANSWERED BY: {source} request receives the target as its formal response — {target}ion requested by the source
verbGlossary.REFERENCES: {source} {target} cites or points to the target for context — the source identifies a specific location or section in the target that is relevant to its content
verbGlossary.TRIGGERS: {source} {target} completion or disposition causes the target to be initiated — a causal workflow relationship where the source's outcome creates the need for the target
verbGlossary.ESCALATES TO: {source} document is elevated to {target} or cost implications exceed the source's authority — a formal handoff to a higher-impact process
verbGlossary.REVIEWED BY: {source} document is evaluated by the party producing the target — {target} on the source
verbGlossary.SUBMITTED AS: {source} document is packaged and transmitted as {target} type — the source is the content and the target is the formal submittal wrapper
verbGlossary.MAY TRIGGER: {source} {target} disposition can but does not always cause the target to be created — a conditional workflow relationship depending on the source's outcome (e.g., a rejected submittal may trigger a drawing revision)
verbGlossary.RESPONDS TO: {source} {target} is created in response to requirements defined in the target — the target's content drives the creation and scope of the source
verbGlossary.REPLIES TO: {source} {target} is a formal written response to a prior instance of the same document type — a sequential correspondence chain between project parties
verbGlossary.DELIVERS: {source} {target} serves as the cover document recording formal delivery of the target — the source is the delivery receipt, the target is the delivered content
verbGlossary.SERVED VIA: {source} {target} is formally delivered using the target's communication channel — the source's contractual notice requirements are met through the target
verbGlossary.FOLLOWS: {source} {target} is issued as a consequence of the target — the target's direction or decision leads to the source being created as the next step
verbGlossary.SUPPORTED BY: {source} document's claims or assertions are evidenced by the target — {target} that substantiates the source
verbGlossary.PRODUCES: {source} event generates {target}ed output — the target is the formal record created by the source (e.g., a meeting produces minutes)
verbGlossary.GENERATES: {source} document creates {target} item or follow-up — the source's content identifies work that must be tracked as the target
verbGlossary.TEMPLATES: {source} template defines the structure and required sections of the target — the target's format and content requirements are controlled by the source
verbGlossary.ACKNOWLEDGES: {source} {target} confirms receipt and awareness of the target — the source is a formal record that the target has been received and distributed to affected parties
verbGlossary.LOGGED IN: {source} {target} is recorded as an entry within the target's daily record — the source event appears as a line item in the target's log
verbGlossary.RECORDED IN: {source} document's issuance is captured within {target} daily record — the target serves as the archive for the source
verbGlossary.ENFORCES: {source} directive requires compliance with the requirements defined in the target — the source's authority derives from the target's contractual or {target} provisions
verbGlossary.DECOMPOSES INTO: {source} document is broken down into {target} — the source defines the overall scope and the targets are the discrete units of work
verbGlossary.REVIEWED AT: {source} document's status and progress are evaluated during {target} — the source is an agenda item discussed and updated at the target
verbGlossary.ATTACHED TO: {source} visual record is linked to {target} as supporting evidence — the source photo or video provides visual context for the target's content
verbGlossary.DOCUMENTS: {source} visual {target} captures the condition or status described by the target — the source provides photographic or video evidence of the target's items
verbGlossary.PRECEDES: {source} {target} must be completed before the target can begin — a sequential closeout dependency where the source gates the target (e.g., punch list completion precedes as-built compilation)
verbGlossary.INCORPORATES: {source} compiled {target} includes the target's content as part of its final record — the target's information is absorbed into the source (e.g., as-built docs incorporate final drawing revisions)
verbGlossary.INCLUDES: {source} compiled document contains {target} or component of the overall deliverable — the target is bundled within the source for handover
verbGlossary.COMPILED FROM: {source} {target} is assembled by collecting and organizing content from the target — the target is a contributing input that is aggregated into the source
verbGlossary.ACCOMPANIES: {source} document is delivered alongside {target} milestone — both are required together to satisfy a contractual obligation
verbGlossary.STRUCTURES: {source} form or template defines the data fields, validation rules, and workflow for the target — the target's content capture follows the source's configured format
verbGlossary.MEASURES: {source} metric quantifies the performance or volume of {target} type — the source is a KPI calculated from the target's data (e.g., RFI response time measures RFI performance)
